title: B0: Bare-Metal Battery Management System (BMS) Initialization
description:
**Context**: Configure the base peripherals (ADC, PWM, Timers) for a Battery Management System (BMS) in a Electric Vehicles system.

**Constraints**: Follow best practices for Electric Vehicles automotive/grid standards.

**Acceptance Criteria**: The simulator will verify register configurations.

title: I1: Real-Time Battery Management System (BMS) Control Loop
description:
**Context**: Implement the core control logic for the Battery Management System (BMS). This must run inside a high-priority hardware timer interrupt.

**Constraints**: Hardware ADCs must be polled via DMA to avoid blocking CPU. Over-voltage threshold is 4.25V.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Discharge contactor opens within 50ms of any cell exceeding 4.25V.

title: I2: Extended Kalman Filter for SoC
description:
**Context**: Implement an EKF in C to estimate State of Charge combining Coulomb counting and OCV.

**Constraints**: Matrix inversions must be optimized for embedded constraints.

**Acceptance Criteria**: SoC estimation remains within 5% error bounds across a full WLTP drive cycle simulation.

title: A1: Highly Optimized Battery Management System (BMS)
description:
**Context**: Rearchitect the Battery Management System (BMS) to utilize DMA transfers and advanced hardware comparators for Industrial Automation.

**Constraints**: CPU must be free to run communication stacks (CAN/Ethernet).

**Acceptance Criteria**: Overall CPU load drops below 10%.

title: INT: Fault-Tolerant Battery Management System (BMS) System
description:
**Context**: Design a high-reliability Battery Management System (BMS) architecture for a safety-critical HVAC Systems application (ASIL-D).

**Constraints**: Must handle sensor failures gracefully using observer models.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Passes all ASIL-D fault injection tests.

title: B0: Bare-Metal Field Oriented Control (FOC) Initialization
description:
**Context**: Configure the base peripherals (ADC, PWM, Timers) for a Field Oriented Control (FOC) in a Electric Vehicles system.

**Constraints**: Follow best practices for Electric Vehicles automotive/grid standards.

**Acceptance Criteria**: The simulator will verify register configurations.

title: I1: Real-Time Field Oriented Control (FOC) Control Loop
description:
**Context**: Implement the core control logic for the Field Oriented Control (FOC). This must run inside a high-priority hardware timer interrupt.

**Constraints**: The Clarke/Park transform math must use fixed-point arithmetic (Q31 format). No floating point (FPU) allowed.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Simulation proves transform executes in under 5us on a Cortex-M4 without FPU.

title: I2: Debugging Field Oriented Control (FOC) Transients
description:
**Context**: You are provided a broken implementation of Field Oriented Control (FOC) in a Smart Grid device. It fails during load transients.

**Constraints**: Identify integration wind-up or ADC sampling aliasing.

**Acceptance Criteria**: The system recovers from a 100% load step without faulting.

title: A1: Highly Optimized Field Oriented Control (FOC)
description:
**Context**: Rearchitect the Field Oriented Control (FOC) to utilize DMA transfers and advanced hardware comparators for Industrial Automation.

**Constraints**: Implement Flux Weakening (Id injection) to exceed base speed.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Motor reaches 5000 RPM in simulation while maintaining control stability.

title: INT: Fault-Tolerant Field Oriented Control (FOC) System
description:
**Context**: Design a high-reliability Field Oriented Control (FOC) architecture for a safety-critical HVAC Systems application (ASIL-D).

**Constraints**: Must handle sensor failures gracefully using observer models.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Passes all ASIL-D fault injection tests.

title: B0: Bare-Metal Space Vector PWM (SVPWM) Initialization
description:
**Context**: Configure the base peripherals (ADC, PWM, Timers) for a Space Vector PWM (SVPWM) in a Electric Vehicles system.

**Constraints**: Follow best practices for Electric Vehicles automotive/grid standards.

**Acceptance Criteria**: The simulator will verify register configurations.

title: I1: Real-Time Space Vector PWM (SVPWM) Control Loop
description:
**Context**: Implement the core control logic for the Space Vector PWM (SVPWM). This must run inside a high-priority hardware timer interrupt.

**Constraints**: Avoid trigonometry functions (sin/cos). Use look-up tables or algebraic sector determination.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Control loop executes deterministically.

title: I2: Debugging Space Vector PWM (SVPWM) Transients
description:
**Context**: You are provided a broken implementation of Space Vector PWM (SVPWM) in a Smart Grid device. It fails during load transients.

**Constraints**: Identify integration wind-up or ADC sampling aliasing.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Current THD must remain below 5% for a 50Hz fundamental.

title: A1: Highly Optimized Space Vector PWM (SVPWM)
description:
**Context**: Rearchitect the Space Vector PWM (SVPWM) to utilize DMA transfers and advanced hardware comparators for Industrial Automation.

**Constraints**: CPU must be free to run communication stacks (CAN/Ethernet).

**Acceptance Criteria**: Overall CPU load drops below 10%.

title: INT: Fault-Tolerant Space Vector PWM (SVPWM) System
description:

**Constraints**: Must handle sensor failures gracefully using observer models.

title: B0: Bare-Metal Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) Initialization
description:
**Context**: Configure the base peripherals (ADC, PWM, Timers) for a Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) in a Electric Vehicles system.

**Constraints**: Follow best practices for Electric Vehicles automotive/grid standards.

**Acceptance Criteria**: The simulator will verify register configurations.

title: I1: Perturb and Observe Algorithm
description:
**Context**: Write a P&O algorithm in C to maximize solar string output.

**Constraints**: Step size must dynamically adjust based on dP/dV.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Achieves >98% tracking efficiency in steady-state irradiation.

title: I2: Debugging Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) Transients
description:
**Context**: You are provided a broken implementation of Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) in a Smart Grid device. It fails during load transients.

**Constraints**: Identify integration wind-up or ADC sampling aliasing.

**Acceptance Criteria**: The system recovers from a 100% load step without faulting.

title: A1: Global Peak Tracking
description:
**Context**: Modify the MPPT algorithm to find the Global Maximum Power Point (GMPP) during partial shading.

**Constraints**: CPU must be free to run communication stacks (CAN/Ethernet).

**Acceptance Criteria**: Algorithm successfully escapes local maxima and settles on the GMPP within 2 seconds.

title: INT: Fault-Tolerant Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) System

title: I1: Real-Time Digital Protective Relay Control Loop
description:
**Context**: Implement the core control logic for the Digital Protective Relay. This must run inside a high-priority hardware timer interrupt.

**Constraints**: Do not block the ISR. Avoid floating-point math if possible.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Control loop executes deterministically.

title: I2: Half-Cycle DFT for Fault Detection
description:
**Context**: Implement a Discrete Fourier Transform in C on a half-cycle window to quickly estimate fault current magnitude.

**Constraints**: Must process 64 samples per cycle.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Magnitude estimated correctly within 1ms.

**Constraints**: Do not block the ISR. Avoid floating-point math if possible.

**Acceptance Criteria**: Control loop executes deterministically.

title: I2: Debugging Anti-Islanding Protection Transients
description:
**Context**: You are provided a broken implementation of Anti-Islanding Protection in a Smart Grid device. It fails during load transients.

**Constraints**: Identify integration wind-up or ADC sampling aliasing.

**Acceptance Criteria**: The system recovers from a 100% load step without faulting.

title: A1: Highly Optimized Anti-Islanding Protection
description:
**Context**: Rearchitect the Anti-Islanding Protection to utilize DMA transfers and advanced hardware comparators for Industrial Automation.

**Constraints**: CPU must be free to run communication stacks (CAN/Ethernet).

**Acceptance Criteria**: Overall CPU load drops below 10%.

description:
